ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ chʋnnana Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa, die ba yiwo Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Andurusi, ta ba taana nɩɩŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma dama die ba yiwo zaasɩyigiriŋ.
translated: Jesus was walking in Galilee, called Simon and Andrew, and they followed him.

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: dɩ nɩ ye ta yɩɩsɩ nine dɩ yuorine, gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna gamɩŋ ye wo gbaamɩŋ, tɩkpaara tieŋ dɩ wʋmma, ta kunti dɩ hagɩna kuŋ me, ta zɔɔlɩntieliŋ dɩ wʋmma Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩnaha.
translated: I cannot provide a translation for this Kma text.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Die ba mana dɩ dii ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ.
translated: I’m sorry, but I’m not able to translate that text.

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Galili a keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma dɩ ʋ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.
translated: I'm sorry, but I don't have the ability to translate this Kma text into English.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: A nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn die dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, vuodiekemba nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa, pɔgɩlɩha aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba juu aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.
translated: The young man named John, who is a farmer from Ngim, lives in the village of Keŋtug, and the people of the village are very friendly, but they also have many problems with the river flooding.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: a balla ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ta ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of Konni to provide a reliable translation.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Womi die ʋ taaŋ ba dɩ ba ga Betilehemi aŋaŋ wɩaha gie: “Nɩ gamma a kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a daansɩ a yaalɩ bʋa wa jigiŋ amʋ nɩ keŋ a ye ʋ benne jigidieke nɩ vaa n tɩbɩŋ wʋŋ amʋ n gbaŋ n ga a jɩaŋ wa.”
translated: Womi said to the people of Betilehemi: “If you do not work hard, you will not have food, you will be hungry, and you will die.”

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Bɩa yine nɩ yile dɩ paanʋ wɩa maŋ bala nɩ? ‘Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dabɔtɩ wɩa!’ ”
translated: Will you go to the market today? “I will go to Farasisi and Sadusisi tomorrow!”

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ ko a vaa vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna wa ko a gbɩ ʋ jayeekiri kʋanʋaŋ; die vuodiekemba mana die dɩ gbɩna ka wa die ye wo gbaanɩŋ.
translated: Those who do not heed advice become foolish, while those who listen become wise; the one who listens is praised, and the one who does not is scorned.

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Hɔgʋba pam dieke die dɩ dɩna Yisa kʋaŋ a nyɩŋ Galili ta suŋŋi wo wo die dɩ zie saasaa a jɩɩma wudieke dɩ yinene wo.
translated: When the people gathered, Jesus went to Galilee, taught them, and many were healed.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Dama Paliti die sɩba a baarɩ haasɩ wɩa die ba yigi Yisa a keŋ yɩ wa.
translated: I told Paliti that the child is sick and we will go to Jesus to pray.

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: a baarɩ “Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ balla ka keŋ wo gbigiye mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha gie yada ta va nɩ wʋbɩatɩ.”
translated: Unable to translate the provided Konni text due to insufficient knowledge.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Womi die ʋ balɩ a kpatɩ ba dɩ, “Vuotaŋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ ama daa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ wɩa ʋ naaŋ vuotaŋ.
translated: When a person goes into the forest and sees a lion, he must run away, but if he runs away, the lion will chase him.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Daaŋ mana sʋkʋʋŋ aŋaŋ yuŋ ʋ ŋaaŋ dɩ chʋŋ wa vɔrɩtɩ aŋaŋ kunkogehe me a nata ta naga tana a gobe ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Konni language to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋsɩ anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Ʋ yi paanʋsɩsɩ a gbieri gbieri ta nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba tuohe a tɩa tɩa a yɩ vuosisi. Die ʋ bɩ nagɩ zaasɩtɩtɩ ale wo a gbieri gbieri a yɩ ba mana mana.
translated: Womi Yisa said that the children who are playing together must be careful, and the elders warned them about danger. If the children keep fighting, the village will be harmed and destroyed. Then the people will be sad and the village will be left empty.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnnana wa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ nan halɩŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ.
translated: I'm sorry, but I don't have enough information to provide an accurate translation of this Konni text.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Die wɩa die ba va daadamba mi ta die a juu haarɩkʋ Yisa die wone bie ka sʋŋ, banɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa dɩ ga. Haarɩsɩ ataŋ gbaŋ die benne mi.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough information about the Konni language to provide an accurate translation of this text.

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Dama a juu wo ʋ nyuuti ma a daaga nyɩŋgbaŋka a nyɩnna ama da ʋ sʋŋ ma.”
translated: I’m sorry, I don’t have enough information to translate this text.

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩnɩŋ lele vuosi gie dɩ wone yada ta bɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ, nɩ yaala n bemme nɩ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaala n yaa wa suguru aŋaŋ nɩ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaa bʋa wa a keŋ giena.”
translated: Yisa said, “If you want to go to the market and buy some food today, why aren’t you coming with me? Will you also bring the children? I’ll be there later.”

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Nɩ a keŋ a tʋgɩ tɩgɩkpɩɩŋ yaa tɩgɩbiŋ, nɩ juu a yaalɩ vuodieke dɩ yaalala ʋ tuo nɩ aŋ nɩ bemme ʋ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan nyɩŋ mi.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Konni language to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo ta kpaaŋ ba, “Nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ a ga vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi buuriŋ yaa nɩ ga juu Samaria vuosi tɩŋ ma.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Konni language to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta ʋ nan nagɩ a yɩ nɩ; nɩ daansɩma ta nɩ nan ye; nɩ kpaasɩma gbɩabɩkʋ ta ʋ nan yuori a yɩ nɩ.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Konni language to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: a dɩ dɩa a pɩasa, “Sɩa ba mɩɩrɩ bʋanyaalɩ dieke dɩ balla ʋ yi Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa? Tɩ ye wo ʋ chɩŋmarɩka dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ, tɩ dɩ a keŋ dɩ tɩ jɩaŋ wa.”
translated: He/she said, “What is your name? Do you want to go to the market today? I will help you later, but first I must go home.”

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: Saŋka mi n nan yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, ‘Wusie n bala nɩ, die nɩ zetine nɩ suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieŋ nɩ nɩmballɩ sʋnsʋŋ wʋnyɩ wa, die manɩŋ nɩ zeti suŋŋiŋ dene.’
translated: Sorry, I cannot translate this Konni text.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Ama nɩ gamma Izara vuosi jigiŋ nyɩɩna, ba sɩna sɩba yii diekemba dɩ bana.
translated: Unable to translate the provided Konni text
